Package microui.core.style

Class LerpedColor

java.lang.Object
microui.core.style.AbstractColor
microui.core.style.AbstractLerpedColor
microui.core.style.LerpedColor
public final class LerpedColor extends AbstractLerpedColor
A concrete implementation of AbstractLerpedColor that uses a condition (or progress supplier) to control the direction of color interpolation.

This is useful for creating color transitions that respond to application state, such as hover effects, selection states, or enabled/disabled states.

See Also:
  • AbstractLerpedColor

    Description

    LerpedColor(Supplier<AbstractColor> start, Supplier<AbstractColor> end, Supplier<Float> progressSupplier)
    Constructs a new LerpedColor with supplier-based start and end colors, and a supplier that provides the animation progress directly.

    Constructs a new LerpedColor with specified start and end colors, and a supplier that provides the animation progress directly.

  • Constructor Details

    • LerpedColor

      public LerpedColor(AbstractColor start, AbstractColor end, BooleanSupplier condition)
      Constructs a new LerpedColor with specified start and end colors, and a condition that controls animation direction. The animation speed is set to 0.02500000037252903f.
      Parameters:
      start - the starting color of the interpolation, cannot be null
      end - the ending color of the interpolation, cannot be null
      condition - a boolean supplier that controls animation direction: when true, animation progresses from start to end; when false, animation progresses from end to start. Cannot be null.
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if start, end, or condition is null

    • LerpedColor

      public LerpedColor(AbstractColor start, AbstractColor end, Supplier<Float> progressSupplier)
      Constructs a new LerpedColor with specified start and end colors, and a supplier that provides the animation progress directly. The supplied progress must be a value between 0.0 and 1.0, where 0.0 represents the start color and 1.0 represents the end color. The animation speed is set to 0.02500000037252903f.
      Parameters:
      start - the starting color of the interpolation, cannot be null
      end - the ending color of the interpolation, cannot be null
      progressSupplier - a supplier that returns the current progress (0.0-1.0), cannot be null
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if start, end, or progressSupplier is null

    • LerpedColor

      public LerpedColor(Supplier<AbstractColor> start, Supplier<AbstractColor> end, BooleanSupplier condition)
      Constructs a new LerpedColor with supplier-based start and end colors, and a condition that controls animation direction. The start and end colors are obtained from suppliers each time the color is evaluated, allowing dynamic color changes. The animation speed is set to 0.02500000037252903f.
      Parameters:
      start - supplier for the starting color, cannot be null
      end - supplier for the ending color, cannot be null
      condition - a boolean supplier that controls animation direction: when true, animation progresses from start to end; when false, animation progresses from end to start. Cannot be null.
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if start, end, or condition is null

    • LerpedColor

      public LerpedColor(Supplier<AbstractColor> start, Supplier<AbstractColor> end, Supplier<Float> progressSupplier)
      Constructs a new LerpedColor with supplier-based start and end colors, and a supplier that provides the animation progress directly. The start and end colors are obtained from suppliers each time the color is evaluated. The supplied progress must be a value between 0.0 and 1.0. The animation speed is set to 0.02500000037252903f.
      Parameters:
      start - supplier for the starting color, cannot be null
      end - supplier for the ending color, cannot be null
      progressSupplier - a supplier that returns the current progress (0.0-1.0), cannot be null
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if start, end, or progressSupplier is null

  • Method Details

    • resetAnimationProgress

      public void resetAnimationProgress()
      Resets the animation progress to the beginning (start color). This immediately sets the interpolation progress to 0, regardless of the current condition or animation state.
